title: 2. Design FMEA Summary
text_content: Appendix A lists all the FMEA items that are linked to Risks from the RMR report and all “Approved” FMEA items that have been analyzed for safety and reliability considerations. Description of Column Headings: DFEMA ID is the unique identifier for the failure. Subsystems is the subsystem that identified the failure mode. Group is the area within the subsystem the failure mode was identified. Context (Component) is a text description of the failing component or assembly. Detailed Context (Function) is a text description of the component’s role/purpose in the subsystem. Part Number is the unique identifier of the component (part or assembly) in the design and manufacturing documentation. Failure Mode is the text description of “how” the component or assembly fails. This is specific manner or way by which a failure occurs in terms of failure of the item (being a part or (sub) system) function under investigation; it may generally describe the way the failure occurs.

title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.2 Integrity > 4.2.2 Software Integrity:
text_content: During the design and development phases of the lifecycle for the application software within Varian medical devices, the integrity of the system build is ensured by controlling the editing process and requiring regular reviews of the code. This is done to ensure that the integrity of the application software is maintained through the construction phase. W hen the software package is ready for release to the user, Varian manufacturing procedures (DND1902) are used to prevent improper or incompatible versions of the software from being distributed or installed. These procedures include measures to ensure the consistency of data, the absence of viruses or malware, and the inclusion of needed user documentation and labeling. For Varian medical devices, installation procedures and updates are handled by trained Varian Service personnel or local Service representatives. Acceptance procedures are utilized to provide a further barrier to loss of integrity, as the

text_content: The newly added Halcyon Evolve model does not involve any changes to the hardware. The update consists solely of a configuration adjustment. Specifically, the Halcyon Evolve is equipped only with the standard kV flat panel detector, while the Halcyon model has two kV flat panel detector options, both standard and high-performance, and MV only config. The detailed change in HW configurations can be found in below table. Difference Previous Current The configurations of Halcyon are Hardware classified depending on the kV detector used: Configuration One: MV Imaging and Standard Imaging kV Flat Panel Detector Configuration Two: MV Imaging and High-performance Imaging kV Flat Panel Detector The Configurations of Halcyon and Halcyon Evolve are classified depending on the Imaging options: HalcyonHalcyon Evolve Configuration One: MV Imaging only / Configuration Two: MV Imaging and Standard Imaging kV Flat Panel Detector Configuration Two: MV Imaging and Standard Imaging kV Flat Panel Detector

text_content: Halcyon 4.0: A variation request or new registration for Halcyon 4.0 is not required to be submitted the TGA. Variations are only required when there is a substantial change to the design, intended performance or quality management system of the device. Legislation does not contain a definition for ‘substantial change’, however, at a minimum, changes to any details on the conformity assessment (CA) certificate constitute a substantial change. These include details such as GMDN code, manufacturer’s details and the manufacturer’s Intended Purpose. *Variants are only required when the design of the medical device has been varied to accommodate different patient anatomical requirements (for example, relating to the shape, size, length, diameter or gauge of the device) or any other variation approved by the Secretary for this definition, if the variation does not change the intended purpose of the

title: 5. Risk Management (Continued)
text_content: There are two high-level categorization of post-market cybersecurity incidents. First, there are cybersecurity incidents that involve the possible exploit of potential Varian product vulnerabilities. Secondly, there are incidents that involve the exploit of vulnerabilities in customer operating environment (non-Varian). Varian has both cybersecurity-specific and non-cybersecurity-specific practices in place to support customers and respond to cybersecurity incidents of both categories in the post market phase. The product security team is involved in both practices. The team acts as an interface to identify incidents where both potential customer environment vulnerabilities and potential Varian product vulnerabilities are at play. text_content: For the most rapid regulatory clearances internationally, the product name on the submission documents needs to match the name to be cleared and used in marketing the device. Upgrades and Conversions: Upgrades from a previous version to HAL 4.0 can be made in some countries where HAL 4.0 is cleared. The details of which countries allow this will be detailed later in the project. For China, the system updates cannot engage any re-labelling activities, the information on device label should not be changed, such as manufacturing date, model name, NMPA license number etc. . Conversions from Halcyon to Ethos radiotherapy sytem can be made in some countries. Conversions involve changes to the covers, installing and enabling the Ethos TPS (On-couch session manager or OSM) function and changing the nameplate to include “Ethos Radiotherapy System” while manufacturing date and serial number remain the same. The UDI will be updated to describe the changed model. Regulatory Plan Revision Doc Number

text_content: device. Reference: Therapeutic Goods (Medical Devices) Regulations 2002. The addition of a new risk control measure does not change the Intended Purpose. Medsafe (NZ) are required to be notified only if there are changes to the information on the WAND database. Similar to the TGA, this includes information such as GMDN code, manufacturer’s details and the manufacturer’s Intended Purpose. Ethos Radiotherapy System 4.0: A variation request or new registration for Ethos Radiotherapy System 4.0 is not required to be submitted the TGA. Variations are only required when there is a substantial change to the design, intended performance or quality management system of the device. Legislation does not contain a definition for ‘substantial change’, however, at a minimum, changes to any details on the conformity assessment (CA) certificate constitute a substantial change. These include details such as GMDN code, manufacturer’s details and the manufacturer’s Intended Purpose.
